title: [InstCombine] Extend fcmp+select folding to minnum/maxnum intrinsics
summary:
The generalization strategy involved abstracting the pattern matching for floating-point min/max operations to handle both ordered (no-NaN) and unordered (may-NaN) comparisons uniformly. Originally, separate matchers (`m_OrdFMin`, `m_UnordFMin`, etc.) were used, requiring multiple checks. The patch introduced combined matchers (`m_OrdOrUnordFMin` and `m_OrdOrUnordFMax`) that unify these cases, simplifying the detection logic.

This change allowed the optimization to apply more broadly, covering all relevant fast-math variants (e.g., `ult`, `ule`, `ugt`, `uge`) without duplicating code. The approach was consistently applied across three components: `ValueTracking` for clamp pattern recognition, `InstCombine` for converting select patterns to intrinsics, and `IVDescriptors` for recurrence analysis.

Key takeaway: When generalizing from a specific test case, identify the core semantic pattern (here, min/max behavior under fast-math) and unify the matching logic to encompass all valid predicate variants. This reduces code complexity and ensures the transformation applies uniformly across equivalent forms.
